data.raceName: DE BORTOLI WINES F&M BM64 HANDICAP
data.raceComments: {"comment":"3. EMPRESS OF JAPAN is a progressive mare resuming, and she usually comes to hand quickly. She was placed once in a shortened spring prep, which is often the case with mares at that time of the year. A couple of tune-up trials will have her ready to rumble fresh. Can win. 5. OCEAN CHILL is heading the right way, and she will be peaking third up over her preferred trip after stalking the speed for a solid second over 1350m at Wyong 10 days ago. The winkers go on, and she has genuine each-way claims. 1. URAFIKI is a tough mare who still has a query over this trip, but she was a strong 1200m TAB Federal winner at Canberra nearly 3 weeks ago when diving through late to score comfortably. Stick with her on an each-way basis. 2. CHILLI MARGS made her Australian debut with a respectable sixth over 1300m here nearly 3 weeks ago. She cops a tough gate, but will be a quick improver, especially if the track is wet. Follow any positive market leads.","runners":[3,5,1,2],"exclusive":true}
